name: Brown Onion Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 pint boiling water and 2 beef extract cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the onion into thin slices.
2. Heat butter in a skillet over medium heat.
3. Add the onion to the skillet and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
4. Pour in the broth (or boiling water with beef extract cubes) and stir to combine.
5. Bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook for about 15-20 minutes or until the onion is tender.
6. Toast the bread slices in a separate pan over medium heat, about 1 minute per side.
7. Place the toasted bread on top of the soup in a casserole dish.
8. Sprinkle grated cheese evenly over the bread.
9. Transfer the dish to an oven preheated to 350°F (175°C).
10. Bake for 5-10 minutes or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
11. Serve hot, portioned for two people.
Source: Cora T. Ruffle
